channel.title: IBD LitMonitor
channel.description: Daily IBD literature monitoring - organized by clinical subfield

item.title: Development and Clinical Validation of an Automated Artificial Intelligence System for Bowel Wall Thickness Assessment in Adult Inflammatory Bowel Diseases.
item.description: Ultraschall in der Medizin (Stuttgart, Germany : 1980) - Intestinal ultrasound (IUS) is used to assess and monitor inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). Bowel wall thickness (BWT) is its key marker, but accurate measurement is operator-dependent and requires...

item.title: Multi-omics-based machine learning model predicts response and guides treatment in Crohn disease: a case study in nutritional therapy.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - Biomarkers are needed to predict treatment response and guide therapeutic decisions in Crohn disease (CD). We aimed to develop and validate a multi-omics machine learning (ML) model to predict resp...

item.title: Deep Learning-Driven Analysis and Quantification of Histopathological features in a DSS-induced Colitis Mouse Model.
item.description: The American journal of pathology - Dextran-based colitis model has been used extensively to study the pathophysiology of inflammatory bowel disease, including ulcerative colitis and Crohn's disease. Histological studies of animal co...

item.title: Integrated Serum Pharmacochemistry, Metabolomics, and Network Pharmacology Uncover Potential Active Components and Mechanisms of Shenling Baizhu Powder in Treating Ulcerative Colitis With Spleen Deficiency and Dampness Stagnation.
item.description: Biomedical chromatography : BMC - This study combined serum pharmacochemistry, metabolomics, and network pharmacology to uncover potential active components and mechanisms of Shenling Baizhu Powder (SLBZP) in treating ulcerative co...

item.title: Strength of Association: How Useful Is Handgrip Strength as a Functional Biomarker in Patients with Inflammatory Bowel Disease?
item.description: Digestive diseases and sciences - Editorial -

item.title: The real diagnostic value of serum iron in iron deficiency anemia among patients with inflammatory bowel disease: A retrospective study.
item.description: The Journal of international medical research - ObjectiveThe diagnostic utility of serum iron in identifying iron deficiency anemia among patients with inflammatory bowel disease remains underreported. This study aimed to evaluate the true diagn...

item.title: Discovery, development, and characterization of SPY002 and SPY072, two novel extended half-life monoclonal antibodies targeting TL1A: in vitro properties, in vivo pharmacology, pharmacokinetics, and preclinical safety.
item.description: mAbs - TL1A is a proinflammatory cytokine in the tumor necrosis factor (TNF) superfamily that signals via DR3 on T helper cells, innate lymphoid cells, and fibroblasts. Dysregulated TL1A signaling has bee...

item.title: Transfer of faeces in ulcerative colitis 2: improving efficacy - study protocol for a multicentre randomised controlled trial (TURN2 study).
item.description: BMJ open - The interaction between the gut microbiota and the host immune system is implicated in the pathogenesis of inflammatory bowel disease, including ulcerative colitis (UC). Targeting the gut microbiot...

item.title: Colonoscopy in elderly patients: diagnostic yield, pre-referral assessment and quality indicators in two Swedish cohorts.
item.description: Scandinavian journal of gastroenterology - Colonoscopy in elderly, frail patients requires weighing diagnostic yield against procedure-related risk, but real-world data are limited. We describe outcomes from two Swedish cohorts undergoing n...

item.title: Association of frail status with incident digestive diseases: A large prospective cohort study.
item.description: Digestive diseases (Basel, Switzerland) - Frailty, reflecting age-related decline in physiological reserve, has been implicated in adverse health outcomes, but its contribution to digestive disease risk is not well established. Leveraging ...

item.title: The trend of obesity and inflammatory bowel disease in Australia and New Zealand: a bi-nation cohort study.
item.description: Internal medicine journal - The paradigm of inflammatory bowel disease is shifting from a malabsorptive, undernourished state to one where obesity is a prevalent comorbidity, yet large-scale Australasian data are lacking. We ...

item.title: Outcomes of Root Canal Treatment in Patients With Autoimmune Disease: A Retrospective Case-Control Study.
item.description: International endodontic journal - The purpose of this study was to compare the outcome of nonsurgical root canal treatment (RCT) in patients with autoimmune diseases (AD) with the outcome in patients without AD. The null hypothesis...

item.title: Advanced therapies for extraintestinal manifestations of IBD: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - Extraintestinal manifestations (EIMs) occur in one-fifth of patients with inflammatory bowel diseases (IBDs) (Crohn's disease[CD]; ulcerative colitis [UC]). As advanced therapies (ATs) for IBD beco...

item.title: Colorectal dysplasia and malignancy outcomes in primary sclerosing cholangitis and inflammatory bowel disease requiring liver transplantation and colectomy: A multicenter study.
item.description: Surgery - Literature guiding management of patients with primary sclerosing cholangitis and inflammatory bowel disease undergoing liver transplantation for primary sclerosing cholangitis and total abdominal ...

item.title: Serum anti-Saccharomyces cerevisiae antibody is associated with disease severity in patients with primary sclerosing cholangitis.
item.description: Digestive diseases (Basel, Switzerland) - Primary sclerosing cholangitis (PSC) is a chronic liver disease accompanying inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). Anti-Saccharomyces cerevisiae antibody (ASCA) is a serological marker associated with ...

item.title: The multifaceted regulation of autophagy protein ATG16L1 and its implications in human diseases.
item.description: Autophagy - Review - ATG16L1 (autophagy related 16 like 1) is a core macroautophagy/autophagy protein essential for autophagosome formation. It also functions in non-canonical autophagy pathways such as LC3-associated ...

item.title: SOCS1 Haploinsufficiency in a Patient with Granulomatous Interstitial Lung Disease and Inflammatory Bowel Disease-like Disease.
item.description: Clinical and experimental immunology - SOCS1 haploinsufficiency is an inborn error of immunity with dysregulated JAK-STAT signaling and heterogeneous autoimmune, infectious, and inflammatory phenotypes; its spectrum and management remai...

item.title: Activation of GABABR alleviates DSS-induced colitis in mice by rebalancing inflammatory responses and antioxidant capacity through IRAK-M.
item.description: International immunopharmacology - The pathogenesis of inflammatory bowel disease is complex, involving key contributors such as inflammatory factors and oxidative stress. While the gamma-aminobutyric acid B receptor (GABABR) is rec...

item.title: Soluble transferrin receptor as a reliable inflammation-independent marker of iron deficiency in Crohn's disease and ulcerative colitis.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - Iron deficiency is a common complication in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), including ulcerative colitis (UC) and Crohn's disease (CD). However, standard iron markers are influenced by inflammati...

item.title: Correction: Human DNA levels in feces reflect gut inflammation and associate with presence of gut species in IBD patients across the age spectrum.
item.description: Microbiome -
